threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus is reliable. Welcome to Dalston, where it’s always five minutes late, even at 3 AM.
Last night on the 343, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Adele while a cat curled up in a seat. Only in South London, eh?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sat at the Neukölln Arcaden when two sparrows landed right on my table, pecking at my leftover Döner. That's when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can literally find a vegan kebab shop next to a traditional döner place. The scent clash alone is worth the visit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their indifference to the BART delays. They know it’s not a matter of if, but when the chaos hits.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy catch a 47 while holding a burrito in one hand and a saxophone with the other. Only in the Tenderloin, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí and cafés, but the late-night bocadillos at local bars are where the real culture thrives.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still scan the Metro map like it's an ancient treasure map, taking way too long to find their stop.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You'll know they're new when they say they love the 'quirky' coffee shop but haven't tried the one on the corner that closes at 2 PM.
Heard two lads on the 133 discussing the best chicken spots in Clapham. One swore by the late-night delivery from that old café on the high street.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard the unmistakable sound of a street vendor shouting ‘Bier! Currywurst!’ while dodging a guy on a bike in the middle of a protest.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find late-night kebab shops blasting techno, where the staff know your order before you even step inside. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid BART at rush hour when the Transbay Tube's a mess. Out-of-towners just wonder why it always feels like a Friday evening commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Noticed a guy with a full drum kit on the 19 bus yesterday, just chilling and jamming while we all tried to pretend it was normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just about Gaudí; they miss our hidden plazas where locals sip vermut, swapping stories well into the night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the crosswalks—waiting too long because they’re not used to the pedestrian lights here.
